text:
There are **4** HIGH-urgency PagerDuty incidents still in the `triggered` state (not yet acknowledged or resolved):

| ID | Title | Service | Created |
|---|---|---|---|
| PD-407 | Checkout latency spikes at peak — page | mobile-bff | 2026-05-14 |
| PD-416 | SSO SCIM provisioning errors — page | billing | 2026-05-21 |
| PD-433 | Image uploads time out over 5MB — page | billing | 2026-05-15 |
| PD-439 | Billing webhook retries drop events — page | search | 2026-05-29 |

**IDs:** PD-407, PD-416, PD-433, PD-439

**Heads up:** 3 of the 4 are on the **billing** service (PD-416, PD-433) or adjacent (PD-407 mobile-bff handles checkout, which calls billing; PD-439 is the billing webhook). Worth checking whether there's a common dependency causing correlated pages before acknowledging individually.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'pagerduty_incidents';"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, title, service, created_at FROM pagerduty_incidents WHERE urgency = 'high' AND status = 'triggered' ORDER BY created_at;"}
